page: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-32 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs derived from 16 physical Intel Xeon cores running at 2.3 GHz, with hyper-threading enabled. The instance is configured with 120 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 3.75 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local SSD storage or integrated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile characterized by weak single-core capabilities but stable multi-core scaling. The single-core CPU performance in stress-ng and Geekbench tests falls into the bottom 10% to 25% tier, and single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression are rated as poor. However, the 32-vCPU allocation elevates multi-core metrics, such as the Passmark CPU score and Geekbench multi-core score, into the middle 50% average tier. Memory bandwidth tests also demonstrate average performance, with read-only large block transfers reaching 101.81 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n1-standard-32 utilizes an older hardware generation, offering a mature resource profile that is suitable for workloads requiring high core counts without demanding top-tier single-thread speed. It is appropriate for multi-threaded database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and moderate-scale LLM inference using small to medium models. It is less suitable for single-threaded processing or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-32 is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 120 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.3 GHz. It contains no local storage or GPU resources. Performance benchmarks indicate a clear tradeoff: single-core CPU execution and single-threaded compression tasks are weak, positioning in the bottom 10% to 25% of tested hardware, whereas multi-core operations, memory bandwidth, and database transactions scale to average mid-tier levels. This hardware profile offers a mature, cost-effective option for multi-threaded workloads that do not require high single-thread velocity. It is best suited for static web serving, multi-threaded database operations, software compilation, and moderate parallel processing tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-32 is an x86_64 general-purpose instance with 32 vCPUs and 120 GB RAM. It offers average multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth, despite weak single-core performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-32 features 32 vCPUs and 120 GB RAM, delivering average multi-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance offering 32 vCPUs and stable multi-core scaling for parallel workloads without local storage or GPU overhead.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs and 120 GB of system memory
Intel Xeon x86_64 architecture running at 2.3 GHz
Average multi-core performance and memory bandwidth
Weak single-core and single-threaded execution speeds
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for static web serving, databases, and multi-threaded tasks
